✨ What does Gen Z really think when they walk into a beauty store? Is it the influence of a trending TikTok? A product recommended by a friend? Or the ritual of self-expression that’s deeply personal? These are just a few of the questions I had the chance to explore during my final semester at IBA, through our Experiential Learning Project (ELP) in collaboration with L’Oréal Pakistan — more specifically with the incredible Asad Kaleem (Consumer Insights Manager) and Maybelline New York as our brand focus. Our project, titled “Understanding Beauty Rituals, Consumer Journey, and Decision Making of Gen Z Consumers,” was a deep dive into the lives, habits, and values of a generation that’s constantly rewriting the rules — whether it’s about beauty, identity, or digital influence. 🔍 From in-depth surveys to on-ground university visits, persona building, and qualitative interviews — our team explored how Gen Z navigates social media, friendships, food, shopping, and self-care rituals, culminating in strategic recommendations tailored for Maybelline New York. None of this would have been possible without the support and brilliance of: • Ms. Farhanaaz, our supervisor at IBA — always guiding us with clarity and kindness • Asad Kaleem, who gave us industry insight and trust in our creative process • And of course, my amazing team: Abir Mansoor, Amira Wasi, and Samia Gohar — each one brought heart, sharp thinking, and energy to the table 🎯 The highlight? Getting to translate raw insights into real brand recommendations that resonate with both Gen Z values and Maybelline’s bold brand identity. It felt like bridging culture with commerce — and it was as exciting as it sounds. This project wasn’t just a requirement — it was a rewarding journey that challenged us, taught us, and left us inspired by the power of consumer understanding.
